Define the target audience - analyze the number of foreign patients in Russia over the past 5-10 years. Based on the opinion of one of the experts, countries such as Latvia, Lithuania, Estonia, as well as the Scandinavian countries - Finland, Sweden can be considered primarily as the target audience of medical tourism in St. Petersburg.
Moreover, when choosing countries for the development of the export of medical services, a number of factors should be taken into account:
Availability / absence of a visa regime
Time required to arrive in St. Petersburg, availability / direct transport links
The duration of waiting for a service in the country, both in the host of medical tourists and in the countries for export
Technological gap, namely the specialization of a medical organization and the level of development of medical technologies in countries
The presence / absence of a price gap when receiving medical services
Health Committee

Conduct a research in order to understand the competitive advantages of each clinic - to determine key medical areas (for example, dentistry, plastic surgery, IVF) that will be promising for the development of medical tourism in St. Petersburg (The rating of private medical organizations in St. Petersburg by main medical areas is given in Appendix 3
Government of St. Petersburg

To create a tour operator of medical tourism (a single provider). The purpose of this provider will be to provide ready-made individual packages of travel services, including medical services, and to simplify the procedure for patient interaction with industry actors
Government of St. Petersburg
Announce a competition or organize a working group to develop a plan for the development of a brand of medical tourism in St. Petersburg
Committee for Labor and Employment of the Population of St. Petersburg

Determine the requirements for medical tourism personnel. Develop a concept for overcoming the cultural (linguistic) barrier by teaching staff in foreign languages and mastering international service standards
Government of St. Petersburg
Initiate the development of a simplified procedure for obtaining a visa for foreign patients, as well as the development of the possibility of obtaining a visa in electronic format
Committee for informatization and communication

To develop an Internet platform for collecting, storing, distributing and exchanging information about treatment in St. Petersburg between foreign patients, as well as for informing foreign citizens about the possibilities of obtaining medical services in St. Petersburg. Also, the Internet platform should integrate information from the websites of medical organizations, as well as organizations of the tourism sector (hotels, transport services, translation services, etc.).

To pass accreditation events for awarding the JCI quality certificate to clinics. In Russia, obtaining JCI accreditation by medical organizations is supported at the state level. Thus, according to the Government order of the Russian Federation, an increase in the volume of exports of medical services can be ensured by obtaining JCI accreditation by medical organizations. In addition, the provision of financial support to medical organizations for JCI certification and others for the licensing of doctors, medical workers is included in the list of priority tasks for the development of the export of medical services. The presence of this accreditation certificate will increase the safety for patients, increase the involvement of staff, which significantly increases the chances of the clinic that a medical tourist will make his choice in its favor.

Membership in international medical tourism associations. Examples of the most popular and authoritative international associations are the Medical Tourism Association, USA), the International Medical Travel Association (IMTA), the European Medical Tourism Alliance (European Medical Tourism Alliance (EEIG). The main goal of such organizations and associations to bring together professionals and specialists of this sphere to discuss their work and to optimize it, for sharing experience and relevant information. Moreover, such membership contribute to protection of consumer interests.

Cooperation with the Russian Export Center. The Russian Export Center is a state institution for supporting non-resource and non-energy exports. The center provides support at any stage of export activities, including through electronic sales channels. Having established cooperation with the regional export center (in St. Petersburg), medical organizations can receive support in participating in foreign exhibition events, in promoting and posting information about the clinic on foreign electronic sites.

Open representative offices of a medical organization abroad. –So, for example, in 2019, the American Clinic in St. Petersburg had a program to open a representative office, a small office in Kazakhstan, since in 2018 there was a large flow of foreign patients from Kazakhstan, and a doctor with a license and certificates from Russia could work and in Kazakhstan. The clinic also considered Baku as a country to open a representative office. However, the idea of opening a representative office in these two countries was not implemented due to the outbreak of the coronavirus pandemic.
Define and establish contractual relationships with counterparties:
- Cooperation with nearby hotels to provide discounts to foreign patients of a medical organization;
- Cooperation with transport companies to provide transfer services
- Using outsourcing medical translation services or do it within the clinic, as part of the creation of an international department

- Organization of catering and other non-medical services, taking into account the religious, cultural, gastronomic preferences of foreign patients. To implement this, the state organization can conclude an agency agreement in accordance with the Federal Law "On the procurement of goods, works, services by certain types of legal entities" dated July 18, 2011 No. 223-FZ.
Medical clinics and international insurance companies collaboration
That lead to attraction of extra financial benefits from other countries and increase the Saint-Petersburg image in a global arena
Increase the number of employees with knowledge of foreign languages

To form an international department in a medical organization (in case its absence), whose duties will be to accompany a foreign patient at all stages of his stay in a medical organization, provide the necessary translations of documentation, register a foreign patient with migration registration, help in placement outside a medical organization, attracting foreign patients, attracting and establishing cooperation with foreign insurance companies, etc.

Increase the availability of information about a medical organization for a foreign patient - create a separate website or section on the existing website "Medical Tourism" in English, as well as in the local languages of target countries, create a feedback form in a foreign language

To form package offers for foreign patients - registration of a complex of medical services in a single package offer. When forming package proposals, one should take into account the special conditions of the visa regime with border states. For example, in St. Petersburg for cruise ship passengers from all countries there is a 72-hour visa-free regime, which is a convenient way for a foreign patient to arrive for a batch examination, short-term diagnostics, and post-treatment control.

Creation of an electronic medical system in which all organizations involved in the implementation of medical tourism could interact. Moreover, the electronic medical system should be able to automatically collect and display statistics on the following data:
- The number of foreign patients who underwent treatment in a medical organization for a certain period;
- Residence of the patient (from which country the patient is);
- The number of medical services provided to a foreign patient;
- Medical direction, according to which the foreign patient was treated;
- The cost of treating a foreign patient;
- Source of financing;
- Other required data

To do the marketing activities - participate in conferences, exhibitions and other events on medical tourism in order to expand the agent network, search for a representative of a medical organization, establish contact with the medical community, communicate with potential patients to further form an offer

Since educational institutions and research centers have a significant impact on the development of the cluster, in this part of the study recommendations will be formulated for educational organizations, particularly, for the Business School of St. Petersburg – The Graduate School of Management, which implements a program for heads of state and non-state health organizations and related markets (manufacturing of medical equipment, pharmaceuticals, pharmacy retail, health insurance, medical education).

As the main recommendation, it is proposed to create a program for the development of medical tourism on the basis of the Graduate School of Management for heads of state and non-state healthcare organizations and related markets to form qualified human resources potential for the development of a medical tourism cluster.

table_of_content.title: Conclusion
text: From the one point of view, the high level of development of globalization, the expansion of the availability of transport systems, the increase of the speed of information flows due to the spread of the Internet and the development of social networks were the drivers of medical tourism development. Furthermore, in price increase for medical care in some states, the unevenness of the quality of medical care provided, as well as the long queues for receiving medical services - all this contributed to the development and dissemination of the direction such as medical tourism.

Medical tourism, based on the provision of planned medical services to foreign citizens, is a promising area in terms of its impact on the development of the city's economy. Medical tourism helps to attract funds not only to the budget of a medical organization, but also to organizations related to the accommodation, transport, and tourism business (hotels, restaurants, translation services, etc.). Moreover, medical tourism contributes to improving the image of the region, as well as the country as a whole.

The health care sector development and modernization of the quality of medical services influence positively the medical tourism development in the domestic market as well as in the international. The development of people movement inside the Russian Federation is also important because the domestic demand influence the quality of medical services provided. Despite the presence of potential for the development of the direction of medical tourism, as well as the increasing demand of foreign patients for medical care in Russia, medical tourism in Russia is viewed as a relatively new phenomenon. Based on the opinion of experts, as well as on a review of the literature, we can talk about the absence of a systematic approach in terms of forming the foundations and subsequent strategies for the development of medical tourism as a separate market with its own infrastructure component, resource provision, state regulation policy, peculiarities of consumer demand, etc.

State support for the development of medical tourism in the region may consist in creating a competitive general tourist infrastructure in the region, in reducing the level of impact of legal formalities associated with the organization of the arrival and stay of foreign patients in the country, as well as increasing the attractiveness and active promotion of various cultural tourist routes. As the main measure for the development of medical tourism in St. Petersburg, the study proposes the organization of cooperative interaction, within the framework of which organizations, independent in organizational, legal and financial terms, unite to achieve common commercial goals, implement joint projects at a local, regional and international level and ensure sustainable competitive advantages in the market. Thus, the creation of a cluster of medical tourism is one of the most effective and promising forms of development of this direction.

Questionnaire for experts 56 I ntro duction A long-established view in regional economic studies is that developing of manufacturing goods is the only way for fast economic growth in developing countries. The significant  amount  of scholars consider that the services industry is unprofitable. Nevertheless, one of the recent studies by Benjamin Faber and Cecile  Gaubert  (2019)  stated that the development of services sector such as tourism have significant long-term effect on rise in complementary industries and as  a  result growth of regional economy.   Tourism is not a clear industry, but a product which consumers could achieve in specific place. The uniqueness of this sector of economy  consists   on  his complexity, because it converting non-traded goods and services in tradable product. Development of tourism market benefit not only for producers and consumers, but also the residents of place where it is developed  ( Luzzi , &  Flückiger , 2003 ) . Moreover, some authors mentioned that tourism positively effect on such regional indicators: “ the increase of the employment rate, greater contribution to GDP, better quality services, higher salaries, larger production, capital generation, higher local budget by collecting taxes, attracting investors in the area ” ( Pascariu ,   Tiganasu ,   2014 ,  P .1100).   On figure 1 we can  observe  that between 2000 and 2019 the number of arrivals in international tourism significantly increased (81%) and this result on strong growth (212%) in international tourism receipts.  The data for this graph was from the report made by World Bank ( 2019) . Figure 1   International tourist arrivals and tourism receipts (% change) Data as of November 2020 Figure 2   Share of GDP gener ated by the travel tourism industry worldwide from 2000 to 2019  ( Statista Research Department, 2021 ) One of the main challenges for the regions in sustainable development is to choose the strategy which will increase their comparative advantages between all the competitors. Policy makers searching for the way to foster regional economic growth have chosen the cluster approach proposed by Michael Porter. The main statement of this theory is that nations or regions could win the competition for market only if their most perspective industries will operate as clusters. The idea of cluster development strategy is that main actors of industry should operate in close proximity.  As stated before, one of the effective mechanisms for increasing the competitiveness and development of the region is the formation and development of dynamically developing clusters. The cluster approach to economic development is used today by the governments of countries such  as the USA, Finland, South Korea, Chile, etc. A considerable amount of literature has been published on the importance of industrial clustering development ( e.g.  Rosenfeld, 1997; Enright, 2001;  Malmberg , and Maskell, 2002). The cluster became an object of research in the framework of both fundamental study in the field of economic geography, agglomeration economics, social network theory, and applied research in the field of urban and regional economics, the national theory of innovation, etc. For the sustainable development of the regional economy, it is necessary to support those sectors that are capable of producing economic and social effects in the long term. Examples of such projects can be found in the field of high technologies, large projects in the industry and infrastructure of the region. The opportunity for the regional economy to rise to a higher level appears in the course of the implementation of projects in which significant investment funds are attracted, new jobs are created, and budget revenues increase.  Medical tourism can act as one of these projects, as it develops  the  healthcare  sector in the country  and creates new jobs.   Despite the fact that medical tourism  has a  developing  trend,  and the number of medical tourists all over the world is growing, the definition of medical tourism,  and  the development of measures for its development requires more detailed consideration. Research   gap Previous researchers focused on description how medical clusters are developed in different regions and countries. Nevertheless, these authors did not explain the causal mechanism of medical cluster development. Although authors explain the effect that the cluster has on several sectors of the economy and society, they did not pay attention on how national and regional context (policy, economy, society, technologies) influences cluster development . Practical research problem Although  t he Russian Federation has opportunities to develop medical tourism (technologies and financial resources), it does not affect the attractiveness for the foreign patients (The Russian Federation takes the 41 out of 46  place  in the Medical Tourism Index in 2020)  The medical tourism cluster development requires the determination of its contribution to the city's economy. Since the city's resources are limited, it is necessary to determine effective approaches to their distribution in the medical tourism cluster development. Research question -  what factors influence the development of the medical tourism cluster in St. Petersburg?  Research goal  - to provide recommendations for the development of a medical tourism cluster for medical organizations and city administration  Research objectives: To define the medical tourism cluster structure (determine the specifics of the structure of the medical tourism cluster). Based on the PEST analysis to identify the factors that determine the medical tourism cluster development. To define the most attractive procedures for medical tourists in St. Petersburg Based on the expert interviews to identify the factors that challenges of the medical tourism cluster development in St. Petersburg. To define strategies for the medical tourism cluster development in St. Petersburg. The research has been organized in the following way.  The first part of the paper  begins by  expla nation of key terms such as cluster approach, medical tourism , and medical tourism cluster.  It will then go on to  description of medical tourism in Russia  and analysis of factors of medical tourism development .  The second part is concerned with PEST-analysis with factors that  have a beneficial effect on the demand for medical tourism in St. Petersburg  and  contribute to an increase in the number of medical tourists , a nd  with  factors  impede the development of medical tourism cluster .  Moreover,   to provide   comprehensive understandings of the medical tourism cluster in St. Petersburg we conducted series of  face-to-face  in-depth interviews  with the heads of medical organizations .  The sample organizations were consciously   selected based on their expertise and experienc e ,   including their experience in promoting medical services, attracting international medical tourists, and   providing medical tourism services.   In the last part of the thesis the set of recommendations for city administration and for heads of medical organizations for the development medical tourism cluster in Saint-Peterburg has been formulated.  CHAPTER 1. MEDICAL TOURISM C L USTER   Cluster approach to industry development The driver of economic growth and an example of an effective tool for interaction between regional geographically close participants in innovation systems is the creation of territorial clusters. Researchers perceive a cluster as a form of connected institutional network that exists in the format of bilateral or multilateral contracts between organizations. Such companies, due to their spatial location and close industry affiliation   are more likely to participate in recurring economic exchanges. ( Шаститко , 2009).  According to the theory of industry organization, a stable system of relations between companies is explained in terms of optimization of production costs, economies of scale, diversity,  specialization  and experience.  ( Teece , 1980). Within the framework of the concept of resource dependence ( Preffer ,  Salancik , 1978), the actions of companies are significantly influenced by external forces,  as  they control key resource flows. The strategic decisions that the company makes are aimed at adapting to external conditions.  To  influence the external environment to make it more favorable, organizations can form alliances. Historically, clusters have been defined as groups of industries linked by flows of goods and services.  ( Czamaski ,  Ablas , 1979).  However ,  M. Porter (1990) articulated and popularized the construct of cluster in his book:  “Competitive advantage of nations” .   The researcher defined clusters as  “ geographic concentrations of interconnected companies, specialized suppliers, services providers, firms in related industries, training institutions and support organizations linked around technologies or end product within a   local area or region”   (Porter, 1990, 78) .   After analyzing the competitive opportunities of more than 100 industries in ten countries, M. Porter (2005)  came to the conclusion  that the most competitive multinational companies tend to concentrate in one country, or even in one region of the country. The author explains this by the fact that after one or several organizations achieve competitiveness in the world market, it begins to spread its positive influence on the suppliers,  consumers,  and competitors.  The   growth   of   one   industry   influences   the   development   of   complementary   industries .   Furthermore , M. Porter  constructed a “competitive diamond”  (figure  3 ) with  f actors that influence competitive performance in international markets. One of the main ideas of Porter’s model that interactions in the  ‘ competitive diamond ’  are more intensive and more effective when firms are located close to each other.    The first stage of cluster development  strategy  ma kes  by policy makers  can be understanding all of these factors .   It is quite important to identify possible cluster’s strengths and weaknesses d uring the first stage of cluster development .  Figure  3   C ompetitive diamond  model  ( Porter, 1990) Using “competitive diamond” we can analyze the competitive advantages of the medical tourism cluster in St. Petersburg. It  is worth  not ing,  the main idea of the model is that  most of the  competitiveness factors are not inherited but created by region  administration .   Factor conditions: S aint  Petersburg has a favorable geographical position (located on the border with Estonia, Finland), as well as high transport accessibility (seaport, airport, major railway junction), which provides geographical accessibility for cooperation with European countries   High intellectual and scientific information potential - about 15% of the country's scientific potential is concentrated in S ain t Petersburg, the presence of leading medical universities  ( e.g.  t he f irst Saint Petersburg State Medical University named after I.I. acad. I.P. Pavlova ,  North-West State Medical University named after I. I.  Mechnikova ) , research centers and laboratories  ( e.g. BIOCAD ,  National Medical Research Center. V.A.  Almazova ) Saint-Petersburg had d eveloped urban infrastructure  Unique cultural center - concentration of a large number of cultural heritage sites The concentration of private clinics and medical centers in S ain t - Petersburg, which confirms the availability of capital investments in the field of medical services Demand   Conditions : According to M. Porter's theory, the needs of the domestic consumer (citizens of S ain t - Petersburg) have a significant impact on the competitive advantage of the region. High domestic demand forms and stimulates the acceleration of the pace of development of organizations in the region. Related and supporting industries Since medical tourism includes both the receipt of medical care and various non-medical services (accommodation, transport, food, etc.), the region needs an appropriate infrastructure. (Suess,  Baloglu , Busser, 2018). Other authors note d  the following positive effects from the formation and development of the cluster (Porter, 1998; Halder, 2002):   improving access to resources, primarily financial  ones  increasing the efficiency of labor use  accumulation of knowledge and dissemination of innovations among its participants  growth of international competitiveness of manufacturers A way of keeping small firms in the face of globalization and increasing international competition. According to Martin and  Sunley ,  (2013, 13) Porter’s definition doesn`t  explain  mention the following issues :  the level of industrial aggregation  at which the cluster should be interpreted ,  what related industries should be included and what activities the cluster participants should do .   Furthermore ,  it is not clear,  the strength degree ties that should be between the cluster organizations  and  economic specialization degree of firms within a cluster. Simultaneously , it worth noting the principal things. T he  definition of  cluster  is not specific and could not  interpreted through  one  universal economic model  of  agglomeration and regional economic growth .  The cluster concept explanation depend s  on   the  areas in which  cluster organizations compete as well as strategies they use  ( Martin  &   Sunley ,  2003 ).  For all that, as  the researchers  ( Van den Berg, Braun and van Widen ,  2001, p. 187) convincingly noted,  a cluster is defined as a network of specialized companies geographically located close to each other (for  example, within the same region, or urban agglomeration), between which the exchange of goods, services, knowledge is developed . Evgeny   Kutsenko  (2015), analyzing the international and Russian experience of existing research on cluster policy, focuses on various aspects of the interaction of participants. In accordance with  this approach,   the  cluster efficiency and  effectiveness  can be  asses  by the quantitative indicators   presented earlier,  and  also by the efforts expended to support the interaction of actors in the implementation of the cluster initiative. Kuznetsova  (2019) examining  clusters and cluster initiatives   in the biopharmaceutical industry  in   the R ussia n Federation,  propose d  to distinguish between existing approaches to the definition of the concept of “cluster” and consider the concepts of “cluster initiative” and “geographical cluster”.  G eographical cluster  defined as  the concentration on a certain territory of enterprises operating in one industry  by the scholars ( Alex androva ,  Ivanova, Kuznetsova , 2019) . Cluster development models The first analyzed model of cluster formation and development was the model offered by Williams (2005). The model contains five stages and twelve steps.  The first stage is the cluster identification around the defined geographical areas.  In the first stage it is necessary to introduce the relevance and prioritize cluster. In the second stage to conduct the initial analysis of cluster activities, its participants and, etc. Furthermore, in this stage there is a need to find a leadership group which will take over the development of the cluster. The third stage is dedicated to the identification of future goals achieving and to creation of the cluster development roadmap.  In the fourth phase the cluster development initiatives are identified. During the last phase ,  the action plans for the cluster development are reviewed and connections are formed between cluster members and other similar or supportive clusters. The other cluster development approach was formulated in the book for the ministry of economy,  labor  and entrepreneurship of the Republic of Croatia government.  In this book there are two models  of cluster development such as “Top-Down” and “Bottom-up” .  In the first model  actors within sectors  formulate a cluster strategy.  Whereas in the bottom-up  approach ,    a  cluster priority list   is provided  in accordance with  cluster   analysis to further the development  (Maxwell, 2013) . The third model was proposed by the International Trade Department of World Bank (2009). This cluster development model consists of four stages: Cluster mapping and initial engagement – identification and engagement with key cluster participants Diagnostics and strategy formulation – the authors provide ten cluster tools to ascertain its competitive position and develop cooperation  between cluster participants and formulate  unified business strategy   The implementation of strategic,  political  and institutional initiatives is to ensure that the key leaders of the cluster are accountable in terms of time, ideas and cost sharing; public-private dialogue on political and institutional obstacles to the implementation of business strategies to increase the competitiveness of clusters. Medical Tourism in the Russian Federation For a long time, Russia did not pay attention to the development of inbound medical tourism. The available indicators were in many ways "spontaneous" and were formed mainly due to the traditional flow of patients from less developed neighboring countries (~ 60% of the flow),  or  former citizens of Russia and their families, as well as due to tourists who were forced to get treatment during  the  trip. As a result, in 2016 only 66 thousand foreigners received medical services in our country, in 2017 - 110 thousand. Systematically,  d evelopment of medical tourism in Russia  started in 2018  with the adoption of  federal  program  "Development of the export of medical services" .   As the part of this program there  is  the  national project "Healthcare" . T he goal of  the pro ject i s to increase the volume of medical services to foreign citizens to $ 1 billion by 2024, and the number of medical tourists to 1.2 million people. At the end of 2018, 300 thousand people used Russian healthcare services (the focal indicator was 432 thousand). The goal for 2019 was to attract 564 thousand foreign patients and the volume of services provided in the amount of 470 million dollars.   In the first half of 2019, according to official data, 530 thousand foreigners used the services of Russian health care, paying 230 million dollars. The definition of medical tourism Firstly, the clarification between the terms medical tourism and healthcare tourism should be made. The definitions of health tourism and medical tourism have  the interrelatedness  ( Hall, 2011 ). Moreover, according to  Karadayi-Usta  and  SerdarAsan  (2020 ), and the United Nations World Tourism Organization (UNWTO) healthcare tourism is an umbrella term  including the subtypes like wellness tourism , thermal health tourism, elderly/disabled tourism ,   and medical tourism.    Many recent studies ( UNWTO, 2018;  Karadayi-Usta  and  SerdarAsan , 2020)  have determined the definition medical tourism -  as a type of tourism “where people often travel long distances to  oversea  countries to obtain medical, dental and surgical care  or  therapy  while simultaneously being holidaymakers, in a more conventional sense”  ( Alberti, Giusti and Papa, 2014, 283) ,  using the de s tination’s inf rastructure, attractions and f a cilities   ( Ile,  Tigu ,  2017).   However, medical tourism is not a journey in order to improve health, but  it is an economic activity  with a merge of at least two economic sectors:  tourism and medicine.  The researche rs (Kim,  Arcodia , 2019)  identified some c ommon characteristics among the medical tourism definitions: medical tourists travel abroad for healthcare based on their own intention, which means that medical tourism is elective and discretionary the services tend to have medical intervention ( e.g.  cardiac surgery or cosmetic surgery), contrary to other forms of health-related tourism products (spa or a massage) medical tourism is applied to the tourists who are not moving within a country but travelling across national borders to access medical services Bookman and Boo kman (2007) pointed out three categories of medical  tourism  services : Invasive  –   in this case  the treatment and recovery  is  fast so patients have time and energy for traveling. Examples of invasive procedures  are  dental   services  and plastic surgery.  Traditionally, invasive procedures  do not  cover   by  the patient’s insurance. Diagnostic  sector  related to blood screening, bone density testing , heart stress tests and electrocardio grams.  Combining preventive health screenings and vacation is becoming more and more  demanded.  Set of services included in  lifestyle medical tourism – wellness, stress reduction, weight loss , antiaging procedures and so on. Thus, based on the analyzed literature , medical tourism in this study is defined as  “ the  phenomenon of travelling across national borders to intentionally access a variety of medical treatments which can be necessary or elective for medical tourists.   These medical treatments can be all services which are offered in hospitals, including serious invasive surgeries such as cardiac surgery, but also light procedures  ( e.g.   cosmetic procedures).   Accordingly, medical tourists are people  who’s  the main goal of entry to Russia is to receive  planned  medical treatment. Medical cluster .  The concept definition One of the approaches to the  medical tourism  development in a country or region is the formation of medical clusters  that is  the formation of various models of interaction between organizations related to the provision of medical care.  Actions  for the patient observation,  rehabilita tion  and treatment  of these organizations (cluster members)  must be coordinated   ( Ковалюнас , 2015;  Комягин , 2011;  Akl  et al., 2015).   Most often, the territory of big medical centers   located  in one territory  with an advanced scientific base and  developed technology   is an excellent and appropriate place for  arising  of associations of competitive organizations  that can be  medical cluster members .   These organizations  is   focused on creating  infrastructure to provi de  the essential resources  for maintaining people health.   Medical tourism cluster may include a wide variety of stakeholders: medical facilities (hospitals,  specialty clinics, cosmetic and   dental , etc. ) , medical professionals,  universities and research institutions, tourism-related industries, and operators (hotels, restaurants, tour operators, travel operators and so on), professional associations and institutes for collaboration (medical equipment, insurance providers), government agencies ( Department/Ministry of Health,   Destination   Marketing Organization, Ministry of Tourism, etc.)  ( Alberti, Giusti ,  Papa , 2014; Stephano, 2012 ) . Factors of cluster development Researchers from the Institute for Statistical Studies and Economics of Knowledge in high school of economics identified three groups of factors of sustainable development of the cluster ( Kutsenko , 2015): External environment and composition of participants;   С ommunication  density and level of self-organization; Strategic orientation on innovation of companies and universities included in the cluster . One of the basic conditions influencing the formation and development of clusters is a developed urban environment, attractive for qualified personnel, offering favorable conditions for innovative entrepreneurship. A developed urban environment means a high level of diversity of professions and competencies, a developed academic environment and  high quality   city  infrastructure. Dominance of large enterprises, narrow specialization of the city, orientation towards government orders are examples of factors constraining the development of a cluster. The wider the presence of companies engaged in specialized in related and supporting activities, and the involvement of relevant specialists in the cluster, the higher the likelihood of innovation creation and diffusion. According to the researchers, successful cluster initiatives usually include from 50 to 100 companies. Furthermore, lack of the necessary number of specialized companies corresponding to the specialization of the cluster initiative can negatively affect the number and quality of jointly implemented projects and lead to increased lobbying for the interests of large participating companies and impede innovative development ( Kutsenko , 2015).  The presence of a sufficient number of companies from interrelated sectors in a cluster is a significant, but not the only condition for its development. An important factor is internal competition, which stimulates improvement, helps to attract the most competitive and push out ineffective actors. It is necessary to maintain competition between cluster members. According to Porter  (1990) , the internal competition of enterprises is relevant for the implementation of the innovation scenario. Innovation develops most effectively if the competition is conducted in equal economic,  social,  and legal conditions. Geographic proximity most often  I mplies similarity in environmental conditions. Researchers identify the density of communication among cluster members as an important factor in the development of a cluster. One of the basic conditions for effective communication is a high level of trust between cluster members. In an effective cluster, partnerships are horizontal  in nature, which implies equal participation in decision-making. The cluster development strategy should reflect an agreed common vision that  takes into account  the needs of all parties. T he authors consider the strategic orientation of companies, as well as universities and research centers within the cluster, towards innovation  a s the third important group of factors in the development of a cluster. It should be  noted ,  that a cluster is not only a tool for achieving goals and implementing existing projects. A cluster is a medium for generating innovative initiatives. Forming such an environment requires the ability to perceive new ideas and forms of partnership, identify weaknesses in a timely manner, show flexibility and encourage interaction. It seems important to support joint innovation projects and reduce the number of stand-alone projects. Researchers  ( Polozhentseva ,  Klevtsova , 2 015)  draws our attention to  the indicators of clusters competitiveness such as: Size - the number of companies and trade turnover the level of geographical concentration transparency and uniformity of business the depth of the value chain - the more complete value chain, the better; clusters usually  incorporates  organizations that provide all the functions within the value chain (manufactures, universities, subcontractors, and etc. the presence of supporting institutions – research centers, educational  organizations  and other institutions it interacts and collaborate with. CHAPT E R TWO.  ASSESSMENT OF THE  DEVELOPMENT OF MEDICAL TOURISM CLUSTER FACTORS IN SAINT PETERSBURG     2.1.  PEST-analysis. It is now well established tha t medical tourism is recognized as one of the most developed and thriving sectors of tourism industry.  The development of medical tourism encourages   the exchange and interaction of knowledge worldwide, which pushes relevant industries in the   medical and tourism sectors as well as the advancement of society.  Furthermore, medical tourism is  a n extremely  important channel to   improve service, create employment, balance international  payments  and thrive on tourism . The creation   of medical tourism  cluster  is one of the most effective ways to develop this direction in the region. However, for the effective operation of the cluster and for the positive externalities of the cluster to spread both to the cluster members and to the regional administration, it is necessary to formulate a cluster development strategy and determine its executors. Before formulating recommendations for the city administration, as well as for the heads of medical institutions, it is  required to   define  the existing  region  possibilities for the  creation and  development of such  medical tourism  cluster, as well as possible obstacles. Thus, in order to determine the opportunities and threats to the development of medical tourism in St. Petersburg, PEST-analysis will be used. In this part of the research ,  I would like to realize which factors  have a beneficial effect on the demand for medical tourism in St. Petersburg, contribute to an increase in the number of medical tourists ,  reduce costs and also improve quality. Such factors are also called opportunities. The Government of St. Petersburg and the heads of medical organizations can take these opportunities into account when developing the direction of medical tourism.   At the same time, there are some  factors   impede   the   development   of   medical   tourism   cluster  –  threats. Such factors as the regional government and the heads of medical organizations should be neutralized.  The determination of these factors will help us formulate recommendations for managers of medical facilities, as well as for regional policymakers. Political factors   The federal and regional support of medical tourism   F or example, creation of a state program, which  contains  the  essential   activities  for the development of medical tourism,  defined  indicators by which it is  thinkable  to assess the effectiveness of th is direction  development ; establishment of focal points) will influence positively to the medical tourism cluster development.  The current  strategy  “F or the development of the export of services until 2025 ”   comprises  a n  explanation  of the Russian export of medical services, priority areas (cardiology, oncology, ophthalmology, neurosurgery), problems and barriers to development (the need to provide financial assistance to medical organizations related to coverage for international accreditation, participation in foreign exhibitions, assistance in advertising and promoting medical services abroad, insufficient level of infrastructure development to attract medical tourists, the presence of administrative barriers associated with the difficulty of obtaining a visa, the presence of language barriers, etc.). This strategy also includes tasks for the development of the export of medical services - overcoming language barriers, providing financial assistance to medical organizations - exporters of medical services, digitalizing the medical sector, promoting Russian medical organizations abroad. However, the lack of indicators of the effectiveness of these measures, the timing of implementation, as well as the ambiguity of the executors of the listed measures hinders the development of the direction of medical tourism. Country's membership in various international unions and associations and availability of international quality certification of medical organizations in St. Petersburg This factor  as well as compliance with international standards of service in the market of export medical services   can enhance the country’s image and   will increase trust among medical tourists   because  the availability of international level certificates guarantees the safety of medical care.  Thus, this  factor  contribute s  to an increase in the number of  international patients.  Rules for obtaining Russian visa One of the most important factors is openness of borders and, also the rules for obtaining visa.  Firstly.  Nowadays, due to pandemic ,  the movement of people to different countries is limited and, probably, it can reduce the medical tourist’s flow.  Secondly ,   the more difficult  (expensive and time-consuming)  it is to get a visa to the country, the fewer medical tourists there will be .   The process of obtaining a Russian visa can often be complicated by the large number of required documents and the waiting time for obtaining a visa.  This can be viewed as a threat to the development of medical tourism that needs to be neutralized.  However, at the same time,  St. Peterburg  ha s  special conditions for the visa regime with border states.  Thus,  In St. Petersburg   a 72-hour visa-free regime is in effect for cruise ship passengers from all foreign countries. This is a convenient way for a foreign patient to arrive for a batch examination, short-term diagnostics, post-treatment control.  So, special  regional conditions can be seen as opportunities for medical tourism development in St. Petersburg.  The bioethical legislation and international travel for abortion services, fertility treatment   The inability to receive (legal restrictions or lack of equipment) one or another medical service in  one’s  own country (for example,  fertility treatment ) may be a reason to enter another country in order to receive the necessary medical care .   So, for example, in China, in vitro fertilization procedures are prohibited, and in Russia they are allowed, which may contribute to the growth of foreign patients. Economic  and political  sanctions against  the  Russia n Federation The imposition of economic sanctions against Russia, as a result of the intensification of the conflict in eastern Ukraine, has led to a sharp decrease in tourist flow to  the  Russia n Federation  from E uropean  U nion  countries  (EU)  in 2014 .  T he presence of sanctions creates a negative perception of the country by foreign tourists, which may be a factor in reducing the tourist flow to St. Petersburg  from the European countries.  EU sanctions have affected a significant decrease in foreign investment in the Russian tourism market, including medical  tourism . One of the consequences of the imposition of sanctions was a decrease in the flow of tourists from the countries of the European Union and the United States. At the same time, it should be noted that the number of medical tourists is constantly growing, despite the sanctions, which is reflected in a significant increase in profits. If we compare the indicators in Russia before the sanctions ($ 9 million in 2010) and after the imposition of sanctions ($ 35 million in 2016), we note that the sanctions did not have a negative impact on the development of the medical tourism sector. The medical tourism sector is developing rapidly, despite all the sanctions of the EU countries and the United States. This result can be explained by the fact that the main flow of medical tourists in Russia is citizens of the C ommonwealth of Independent States  (C IS )  countries (70%), which still consider Russia  as  a priority destination for receiving medical services (Data in Figure 5). Thus, according  to the Government order of the Russian Federation , an increase in the volume of exports of medical services can be ensured by obtaining JCI accreditation by medical organizations. In addition, the provision of financial support to medical organizations for JCI certification and others for the licensing of doctors, medical workers  is  included in the list of priority tasks for the development of the export of medical services. The presence of this accreditation certificate will increase the safety for patients, increase the involvement of staff, which significantly increases the chances of the clinic that a medical tourist will make his choice in its favor. Membership in international medical tourism associations.  Examples of the most popular and authoritative  international associations are the Medical Tourism Association ,  USA),  the  International Medical Travel Association (IMTA), the European Medical Tourism Alliance (European Medical Tourism Alliance  ( EEIG) . The main goal of such organizations and associations to bring together professionals and specialists  of this sphere to discuss their work and  to optimize it, for sharing exper ience and relevant information. Moreover, such membership  contribute  to protection of consumer interests.  Cooperation with the Russian Export Center .  The Russian Export Center is a state institution for supporting non-resource and non-energy exports. The center provides support at any stage of export activities, including through electronic sales channels. Having established cooperation with the regional export center (in St. Petersburg), medical organizations can receive support in participating in foreign exhibition events, in promoting and posting information about the clinic on foreign electronic sites. Open representative offices of a medical organization abroad . –So, for example, in 2019, the American Clinic in St. Petersburg had a program to open a representative office, a small office in Kazakhstan, since in 2018 there was a large flow of foreign patients from  Kazakhstan, and a doctor with a license and certificates from Russia could work and in Kazakhstan. The clinic also considered Baku as a country to open a representative office. However, the idea of opening a representative office in these two countries was not implemented   due to the outbreak of the coronavirus pandemic. Define and establish contractual relationships with counterparties : - Cooperation with nearby hotels to provide discounts to foreign patients of a medical organization; - Cooperation with transport companies to provide transfer services -  Using o utsourcing medical translation services or  do it  within the clinic, as part of the creation of an international department - Organization of catering and other non-medical services,  taking into account  the religious, cultural, gastronomic preferences of foreign patients .  To implement this, the state organization can conclude an agency agreement in accordance with the Federal Law "On the procurement of goods, works, services by certain types of legal entities" dated July 18, 2011 No. 223-FZ . Medical clinics and international insurance  companies  c ollaboration   That l ead to attraction of extra financial benefits from other countries and  increase the S aint-Petersburg image in a global arena Increase the number of employees with knowledge of foreign languages To form an international department in a medical organization  (in case  its  absence), whose duties will be to accompany a foreign patient at all stages of his stay in a medical organization, provide the necessary translations of documentation, register a foreign patient with migration registration, help in placement outside a medical organization, attracting foreign patients,  attracting  and establishing cooperation with foreign insurance companies, etc. Increase the availability of information about a medical organization for a foreign patien t -  c reate a separate website or section on the existing website "Medical Tourism" in English, as well as in the local languages of target countries, create a feedback form in a foreign language To form package offers for foreign patients  - registration of a complex of medical services in a single package offer .  When forming package proposals, one should  take into account  the special conditions of the visa regime with border states. For example, in St. Petersburg for cruise ship passengers from all countries there is a 72-hour visa-free regime, which is a convenient way for a foreign patient to arrive for a batch examination, short-term diagnostics, and post-treatment control. Creation of an electronic medical system in which all organizations involved in the implementation of medical tourism could interact. Moreover, the electronic medical system should be able to automatically collect and display statistics on the following data: - The number of foreign patients who underwent treatment in a medical organization for a certain period; - Residence of the patient (from which country the patient is); - The number of medical services provided to a foreign patient; -  Medical direction , according to which the foreign patient was treated; - The cost of treating a foreign patient; - Source of financing; - Other required data To d o  the  marketing activities   -  p articipat e  in conferences,  exhibitions  and other events on medical tourism in order to expand the agent network, search for a representative of a medical organization, establish contact with the medical community, communicate with potential patients to further form an offer   Recommendations for the development of medical tourism cluster to the heads of  Universities and educational program Since educational institutions and research centers have a significant impact on the development of the cluster, in this part of the study recommendations will be formulated for educational organizations,  particularly,  for the Business School of St. Petersburg  –  The Gr aduate School of Management , which implements a program for heads of state and non-state health organizations and related markets (manufacturing of medical equipment, pharmaceuticals, pharmacy retail, health insurance, medical education). As the main recommendation, it is proposed to create a program for the development of medical tourism on the basis of the Graduate School of Management for heads of state and non-state healthcare organizations and related markets to form qualified human resources  potential  for the development of a medical tourism cluster. Conclusion From the one point of view, t he high level of development of globalization, the expansion of the availability of transport systems, the increase  of  the speed of information flows due to the spread of the Internet and the development of social networks   were the drivers of medical tourism development.  Furthermore ,  in pric e increase  for medical care in some  states , the unevenness of the quality of medical care provided, as well as the  long  queues for receiving medical services - all this contributed to the development  and  dissemination  of the direction  such as  medical tourism. Medical tourism, based on the provision of planned medical services to foreign citizens, is a promising area in terms of its impact on the development of the city's economy. Medical tourism helps to attract funds not only to the budget of a medical organization, but also to organizations related to the   accommodation, transport ,  and  tourism business (hotels, restaurants, translation services, etc.). Moreover, medical tourism contributes to improving the image of the region, as well as the country as a whole. The  health care sector development and modernization of the  quality of medical services influence positively the  medical tourism  development  in the domestic market as well as in the international.  The development of people movement  inside the Russian Federation is also important  because the domestic demand influence the quality of medical  services provided.   Despite the presence of potential for the development of the direction of medical tourism, as well as the increasing demand of foreign patients for medical care in Russia, medical tourism in Russia is viewed as a relatively new phenomenon. Based on the opinion of experts, as well as on a review of the literature, we can talk about the absence of a systematic approach in terms of forming the foundations and subsequent strategies for the development of medical tourism as a separate market with its own infrastructure component, resource provision, state regulation policy, peculiarities of consumer demand, etc. State support for the development of medical tourism in the region may consist in creating a competitive general tourist infrastructure in the region, in reducing the level of impact of legal formalities associated with the organization of the arrival and stay of foreign patients in the country, as well as increasing the attractiveness and active promotion of various cultural tourist routes.   As the main measure for the development of medical tourism in St. Petersburg, the study proposes the organization of cooperative interaction, within the framework of which organizations, independent in organizational,  legal  and financial terms, unite to achieve common commercial goals, implement joint projects at a local, regional and international level and ensure sustainable competitive advantages in the market. Thus, the creation of a cluster of medical tourism is one of the most effective and promising forms of development of this  direction .
